key_insight: A two-level Schwarz coarse solver with a structured, non-nested global coarse space can replace AMG as the p-multigrid coarse solve for Poisson problems.
engineering_relevance: This offers a scalable alternative for pressure Poisson solves in spectral/fem incompressible Navier-Stokes codes on exascale machines.
method: Local solve in the original p-multigrid coarse space
Global coarse problem built from a novel structured, non-nested coarse space
Communication-free interpolation between the two coarse spaces
evidence_signal: The abstract reports comparative experiments against BoomerAMG using Nek5000/RS on Summit and Frontier, indicating evaluation on highly scalable production solvers and supercomputers.
watch_item: The approach depends on carefully designed coarse-space structure, so performance may be implementation- and discretization-dependent.
paper_title: CoarseSolvers for Exascale Solution of Poisson Problems
